title: Please Enter Your Email Address* (Your digital master diploma will be sent here for you to have color print out  on 100# White Card Stock)
Please enter your Full Name as you wish it to appear on your ICA Diploma.

Question 1. Dementia Intelligence™ is the study of interactions and how to best improve communication between the person living with dementia and the care provider; as well as the introduction to The Psychology of the Dementia Brain, Lord's Theory.*
Question 2   Please select the best response that applies to a person living with dementia:

Question 3: The TDI system establishes a new culture in the organization, from top to bottom. It includes, but is not limited to, such topics as “dialect”, a new position as a Dementia Coach in a leadership position, increased interprofessional interactions and exchanges, and includes family caregivers as contributing members of the TDI care team.
Question 4: The new TDI culture is predicted on keeping the status quo but adding a new position in the organizational chart that we call the Dementia Coach.
Question 5: The concept of Dementia Intelligence™, which includes the study of The Psychology of the Dementia Brain,  as introduced by the ICA, is furthered defined as:
Question 6: Under the TDI Model, the following staffing changes are integral to the success of the program:

Question 7:  The Dementia Coach takes full responsibility for the competency and morale levels of the care team and employees. The Dementia Coach also serves as a liaison between care providers, treatment staff (i.e.) MD, PT, OT, etc.) and support staff (kitchen, drivers, janitors), and top management so that admin and caregiving teams are in sync at all times. The Dementia Coach is TDI Certified (Fundamentals, Advanced, Coaching, and Dementia Connection (TM), as both an expert in the TDI Model of care and specially trained in direct, personal coaching skills, and more.
Question 8: Meaningful activities improve self-esteem and well-being for the person living with dementia and results in a sense of accomplishment and belonging.

Question 9: The three elements of engagement include the following:  
Cognitive Engagement: This refers to the mental and intellectual focus and absorption in the work, including attention, understanding, and the application of knowledge and skills.  
Emotional Engagement: This involves the feelings and emotions associated with the work and the organization, such as passion, excitement, and a sense of belonging on purpose. 
Behavioral Engagement: This encompasses the actions and behaviors that demonstrate commitment and involvement, such as going above and beyond, actively participating, and staying on task.

Question 10: Touch is very important for someone who welcomes touching. Not everyone living with dementia will accept touching. Please select the touches that are most often practiced in the course of providing care to a person living with dementia.

Question 11: There are two other major themes, and two minor themes, of engagement in the fieldwork article entitled Definitions and Measurement of Engagement in Persons with Dementia by William Matcham, et al (2022). These served to capture affect, attitude, and attention of the participant. What were the two major themes?
Question 12: The left side of the human brain is more dominant in a person living with dementia?

Question 14: The left brain is the hemisphere of the brain that controls logic, verbal comprehension and time. So we might expect major losses of these faculties in the mid-to-late stages of dementia. (Parent Ego State.)
Question 15: The right brain hemisphere controls areas such as the emotions, visualization, and creativity. (Child Ego State.)
Question 16: If word choices offer the least impact in communication, what inferences could we draw from that in the field of dementia caregiving?
Question 17: In total, how many personalities can an individual display at anytime (please include effective and ineffective personalities)?
Question 18: What do we mean by symbiosis in The Psychology of the Dementia Brain in the TDI Model.

Question 19: Why is it vital for the Dementia Coach to know all about symbiosis with the ability to relate and teach others on the care team whether at home, in adult day care, assisted living or skilled nursing?

Question 20: Calling the person living with dementia by their first name brings them into the present--and adding "listen to me" captures their active attention. (Adult Ego State). What additional two tips does the short film (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-XFm5pVL-zg), recommend to do?
Question 21: What happens when a care provider poses a question to the person living with dementia that requires thinking and/or reasoning?
BONUS Question 1: Why is the book TA TODAY: A New Introduction to Transactional Analysis by Ian Stewart and Vann Jones so relevant to successful dementia care?
BONUS Question 2: What is Functional Symbiosis and how does it work?
